commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=11cce4758cdace7ef2a5a3bf71892d10d97888fe)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds six curated OpenRouter image models, routes them through OpenRouter's `/images` API with `data[].b64_json` parsing, and updates focused provider tests and OpenRouter docs.

PR surface: Source +84, Tests +44, Docs +13. Total +141 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: PR head sends a shared Images API body while live OpenRouter model records show model-specific unsupported fields. I did not run a paid generation request.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor proof plus a maintainer-visible provider capability decision, not a safe automated takeover of the PR branch.

target.title: [codex] Add current OpenRouter image models
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Related #91504.

OpenRouter image generation currently exposes only a small static shortcut list, so users asking for broader OpenRouter image model support do not see current image-output models in OpenClaw's provider metadata or generated model catalog. Current OpenRouter image models are also documented under OpenRouter's dedicated Images API, so exposing them through the old chat-completions path would advertise models that can fail at runtime.

## Why This Change Was Made

OpenRouter's public `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/images/models` response currently advertises additional image models beyond the existing OpenClaw list, including:

- `google/gemini-3.1-flash-image`
- `google/gemini-3-pro-image`
- `google/gemini-2.5-flash-image`
- `openai/gpt-5-image`
- `openai/gpt-5-image-mini`
- `microsoft/mai-image-2.5`

This PR adds those current model ids to the curated OpenRouter image-generation provider list, routes them through OpenRouter's dedicated `/images` endpoint, and parses the Images API `data[].b64_json` response shape. Existing preview/default chat-image model refs remain accepted on the chat-completions path so already configured users are not migrated unexpectedly.

This intentionally remains a curated-list update, not dynamic catalog discovery; the broader catalog policy can still be considered separately.

## User Impact

Users can now discover and configure more current OpenRouter image models for the `image_generate` tool without relying on undocumented override strings. The linked issue's `microsoft/mai-image-2.5` example is included, and existing OpenRouter image configs continue to work because the previous preview ids remain in the list and the default is unchanged.

## Evidence

- OpenRouter docs: current image generation uses `POST /api/v1/images` and returns image data through `data[].b64_json`: https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/overview/multimodal/image-generation
- Queried OpenRouter's live `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/images/models` endpoint on 2026-06-28 and confirmed:
  - `google/gemini-3.1-flash-image: present`
  - `google/gemini-3-pro-image: present`
  - `google/gemini-2.5-flash-image: present`
  - `openai/gpt-5-image: present`
  - `openai/gpt-5-image-mini: present`
  - `microsoft/mai-image-2.5: present`
  - `image model count: 38`
- Local provider-list proof from `buildOpenRouterImageGenerationProvider()` on 2026-06-28 confirmed all six ids above are listed; `provider model count: 9`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/openrouter/image-generation-provider.test.ts`
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/openrouter/image-generation-provider.ts extensions/openrouter/image-generation-provider.test.ts docs/providers/openrouter.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm docs:list`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --codex-bin /tmp/codex-fast-wrapper` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

Proof gap: I did not run a paid live image generation request against OpenRouter. The runtime endpoint/body/response path is covered by focused provider tests plus the public OpenRouter model discovery endpoint and official Images API docs.
target.author: aditya-vithaldas

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
